significance.button_name: Check failed element with Playwright MCP
significance.purpose: Diagnose the exact failed element/action using observable browser/accessibility/DOM evidence before any code patch.
what_it_identifies: the failed locator/action candidate from Playwright error text
whether the intended text/role/testId is visible in the current GUI evidence
whether the element is missing, detached from DOM, hidden, disabled, duplicated, inside iframe/shadow DOM, or blocked by overlay/permission popup
which pageObject/page/helper file should own the smallest safe fix
significance.what_it_does_not_do: It does not silently change files. It creates RCA evidence and a safe patch direction for Create safe fix plan / Fix failed tests safely.

pom_locator_mapping.pom_strategy_observation: Prefer fixing locator in pageObjects/locator module or reusable page method. Inline spec locator changes require human review unless no POM layer exists.

mcp_auditable_rca_steps.name: Identify failed locator/action
mcp_auditable_rca_steps.question: Which locator or Playwright action failed?
evidence: "message": "TimeoutError: locator.waitFor: Timeout 10000ms exceeded.\nCall log:\n\u001b[2m  - waiting for getByRole('button', { name: /^log.?in$/i }).or(getByRole('link', { name: /^log.?in$|^login$/i })).or(locator('button:has-text(\\'Log in\\'), button:has-te
mcp_auditable_rca_steps.mcp_usage: Use Playwright MCP accessibility snapshot around the current page or failed state to compare the target role/name/text with the failed locator.

mcp_auditable_rca_steps.name: Check locator text on visible GUI
mcp_auditable_rca_steps.question: Is the intended button/link/input text visible to the user?
mcp_auditable_rca_steps.mcp_usage: Use MCP browser snapshot / accessibility tree to list visible roles and names. Prefer role+accessible-name over screenshot guessing.

mcp_auditable_rca_steps.name: Check DOM/accessibility presence
mcp_auditable_rca_steps.question: Does the element exist in DOM/accessibility tree?
mcp_auditable_rca_steps.mcp_usage: Use MCP snapshot and locator probe to confirm whether the target exists, is hidden, duplicated, or inside iframe/shadow DOM.

mcp_auditable_rca_steps.name: Check actionability/interactability
mcp_auditable_rca_steps.question: If element exists, can it receive click/fill/select action?
mcp_auditable_rca_steps.mcp_usage: Use MCP click/hover/fill probes only in diagnostic mode. Detect overlays, disabled state, offscreen location, detached DOM, and permission popups.

mcp_auditable_rca_steps.name: Map fix to POM/reuse layer
mcp_auditable_rca_steps.question: Is the correct locator strategy implemented in pageObjects/pages/util layer?
mcp_auditable_rca_steps.mcp_usage: Use observed role/name/testId from MCP plus RAG-indexed framework context. Patch pageObjects first, then page methods/helpers, not raw spec locators.

mcp_assisted_locator_rca.safe_fix_rule: Use MCP/accessibility snapshot evidence to repair the smallest POM-layer locator/action fix. Do not weaken assertions, skip tests, force click by default, or patch passed scripts.
mcp_assisted_locator_rca.memory_note: This MCP-assisted diagnosis is saved so future Codex/Ollama prompts can reuse observed locator, UI text, DOM, and POM mapping evidence.

auditable_rca_reasoning_checklist.name: Auditable RCA reasoning checklist
auditable_rca_reasoning_checklist.privacy_note: This is a human-readable checklist of observable evidence and decisions, not hidden chain-of-thought.

checks.check: Confirm failure belongs to selected failed specs only
checks.fix_decision: RCA/self-healing scope is restricted to failed specs and imported files only.

checks.check: Check Playwright/Node module resolution and TypeScript path aliases
checks.fix_decision: If present, fix tsconfig paths/package/Playwright runtime preload first. Do not run DOM locator MCP as the primary RCA for Cannot find module errors.

checks.check: Check whether failed locator or expected element exists in DOM/accessibility snapshot
observable_evidence: "message": "TimeoutError: locator.waitFor: Timeout 10000ms exceeded.\nCall log:\n\u001b[2m  - waiting for getByRole('button', { name: /^log.?in$/i }).or(getByRole('link', { name: /^log.?in$|^login$/i })).or(locator('button:has-text(\\'Log in\\'), button:has-te
checks.fix_decision: If absent, update pageObject locator or treat as product/data/environment issue before patching assertions.

checks.check: Validate locator strategy/address correctness
checks.observable_evidence: Look for strict-mode ambiguity, brittle nth selectors, stale text, role/name mismatch, data-testid changes, iframe/shadow DOM.
checks.fix_decision: Prefer stable getByRole/getByTestId/getByLabel in pageObjects; keep framework style.

checks.check: Check interactability: visible, enabled, stable, not detached
checks.observable_evidence: Playwright actionability logs, trace, screenshot, video, error-context.md.
checks.fix_decision: If locator is detached/not attached to DOM, use Playwright MCP/codegen/live DOM snapshot to generate a current locator, then patch pageObject or page method to re-query after DOM settles. Do not add blind waitForTimeout.

checks.check: Check viewport/page-size/scroll issue
checks.observable_evidence: Element may exist lower on page, behind footer/header, or in mobile drawer based on screen size.
checks.fix_decision: Use scrollIntoViewIfNeeded, viewport-aware helper, responsive locator, or mobile-specific page method.

checks.check: Check overlay, popup, permission dialog, modal interception
checks.observable_evidence: Pointer-event interception logs, modal/header/body identifiers, screenshots/videos.
checks.fix_decision: Dismiss/handle overlay in shared helper before click; avoid force:true by default.

checks.check: Check navigation/state synchronization
checks.observable_evidence: URL mismatch, repeated same URL, skipped navigation, timeout waiting for state.
checks.fix_decision: Patch page navigation helper with deterministic state/URL waits; avoid blind sleeps.

checks.check: Check test data, auth/session, API/backend, VPN/proxy/environment
checks.observable_evidence: Network/API errors, login not visible, account feature not available, blocked endpoints, timeout.
checks.fix_decision: Do not fake pass. Validate environment/data first; patch only if framework wait/login helper is unstable.

checks.check: Check assertion/product behavior drift
checks.observable_evidence: Expected vs received mismatch and screenshots.
checks.fix_decision: Only update assertions after confirming product behavior changed and human/business review allows it.

checks.check: Select safest self-healing patch location
checks.fix_decision: Patch config/package/runtime alias setup for module-resolution failures; patch locator/pageObjects only for browser DOM failures.

auditable_rca_reasoning_checklist.summary: RCA first checks Playwright/Node module resolution and tsconfig aliases, then locator existence, locator strategy, interactability, viewport/scroll, overlays/popups, navigation, environment/data, and assertion drift before recommending a patch.

aut_understanding.note: This is static AUT understanding from tests/config/docs. MCP/headed execution adds runtime page/accessibility evidence after failures.

architecture_recommendations: Brittle locator ratio is high. Use MCP/accessibility evidence to migrate unstable locators carefully in related failed scope.
Before any automatic fix, resolve failed spec dependency chain and patch only the related spec/page/pageObject/helper/testData files.

rca_self_healing_policy.explain_failed_tests: Use execution logs, failed inventory, dependency chain, RAG chunks, MCP evidence and framework memory to classify root cause.
rca_self_healing_policy.check_failed_element_with_mcp: Use runtime accessibility/DOM/actionability evidence for the failed locator/action; does not modify files.

spec file only when no reusable layer exists

cache_storage_policy.why: Existing-framework learning follows the user-selected framework path. Central files are compatibility mirrors for the GUI only.

ai.message: Deterministic RCA signals used. Select Codex/Ollama for extra failure reasoning.

recommended_fix_plan: Patch pageObjects/locator modules first when locator is missing or unstable.
Patch reusable page methods/BasePage/helpers when interaction, overlay, scroll, wait, or navigation is unstable.
Avoid raw locator fixes inside specs unless the framework has no reusable layer for that screen.
After patch, rerun failed specs only from this inventory.
Use multi-signal RCA before patching: DOM snapshot diff, trace replay/timing, HAR diff, fixture/seed diff, and cross-run flakiness frequency.
Run assertion drift classifier before updating any assertion; semantic similarity below 0.30 or behavioral/numeric changes must require human review.
Run second-stage patch confidence review; below 0.80 must require human approval and must not leave an auto-applied patch active.
strict_rules: Existing framework mode never generates new functional testcases.
Already-passed specs are preserved and not rerun during failed-only validation.
Second-stage confidence gate blocks auto-apply below 0.80.

what_likely_happened: The test most likely waited for an element that was missing, unstable, hidden, or matched by an outdated locator.
The failure may be an assertion difference. Changing the expected value should require manual approval unless evidence proves it is a harmless copy update.
plain_english_failure_report.safe_next_action: Apply self-healing only if the policy gate allows it; otherwise raise manual review/product/environment issue.
recommended_human_steps: Open the Playwright trace and screenshot for the failed spec.
Check whether the failed locator belongs in pageObjects rather than the spec file.
Check API status/auth/session/test data before patching UI flow.
Review Codex patch diff; reject assertion weakening, test skips, hard waits, and force clicks.
Rerun failed specs only, then merge the retry result into the original report.

auditable_rca_reasoning_checklist.name: Auditable RCA reasoning checklist
auditable_rca_reasoning_checklist.privacy_note: This is a human-readable checklist of observable evidence and decisions, not hidden chain-of-thought.

checks.check: Confirm failure belongs to selected failed specs only
checks.fix_decision: RCA/self-healing scope is restricted to failed specs and imported files only.

checks.check: Check Playwright/Node module resolution and TypeScript path aliases
checks.fix_decision: If present, fix tsconfig paths/package/Playwright runtime preload first. Do not run DOM locator MCP as the primary RCA for Cannot find module errors.

checks.check: Check whether failed locator or expected element exists in DOM/accessibility snapshot
observable_evidence: "message": "TimeoutError: locator.waitFor: Timeout 10000ms exceeded.\nCall log:\n\u001b[2m  - waiting for getByRole('button', { name: /^log.?in$/i }).or(getByRole('link', { name: /^log.?in$|^login$/i })).or(locator('button:has-text(\\'Log in\\'), button:has-te
checks.fix_decision: If absent, update pageObject locator or treat as product/data/environment issue before patching assertions.

checks.check: Validate locator strategy/address correctness
checks.observable_evidence: Look for strict-mode ambiguity, brittle nth selectors, stale text, role/name mismatch, data-testid changes, iframe/shadow DOM.
checks.fix_decision: Prefer stable getByRole/getByTestId/getByLabel in pageObjects; keep framework style.

checks.check: Check interactability: visible, enabled, stable, not detached
checks.observable_evidence: Playwright actionability logs, trace, screenshot, video, error-context.md.
checks.fix_decision: If locator is detached/not attached to DOM, use Playwright MCP/codegen/live DOM snapshot to generate a current locator, then patch pageObject or page method to re-query after DOM settles. Do not add blind waitForTimeout.

checks.check: Check viewport/page-size/scroll issue
checks.observable_evidence: Element may exist lower on page, behind footer/header, or in mobile drawer based on screen size.
checks.fix_decision: Use scrollIntoViewIfNeeded, viewport-aware helper, responsive locator, or mobile-specific page method.

checks.check: Check overlay, popup, permission dialog, modal interception
checks.observable_evidence: Pointer-event interception logs, modal/header/body identifiers, screenshots/videos.
checks.fix_decision: Dismiss/handle overlay in shared helper before click; avoid force:true by default.

checks.check: Check navigation/state synchronization
checks.observable_evidence: URL mismatch, repeated same URL, skipped navigation, timeout waiting for state.
checks.fix_decision: Patch page navigation helper with deterministic state/URL waits; avoid blind sleeps.

checks.check: Check test data, auth/session, API/backend, VPN/proxy/environment
checks.observable_evidence: Network/API errors, login not visible, account feature not available, blocked endpoints, timeout.
checks.fix_decision: Do not fake pass. Validate environment/data first; patch only if framework wait/login helper is unstable.

checks.check: Check assertion/product behavior drift
checks.observable_evidence: Expected vs received mismatch and screenshots.
checks.fix_decision: Only update assertions after confirming product behavior changed and human/business review allows it.

checks.check: Select safest self-healing patch location
checks.fix_decision: Patch config/package/runtime alias setup for module-resolution failures; patch locator/pageObjects only for browser DOM failures.

auditable_rca_reasoning_checklist.summary: RCA first checks Playwright/Node module resolution and tsconfig aliases, then locator existence, locator strategy, interactability, viewport/scroll, overlays/popups, navigation, environment/data, and assertion drift before recommending a patch.
